2606.14306.title: Thinking Outside the [Chat]Box: Bridging Computer Science and Industrial Design for Cognitive-Inclusive Generative AI
2606.14306.paper_summary: Current Generative AI (GenAI) interfaces remain largely constrained to chatbox interaction, which can impose high cognitive demands on users and create substantial barriers for people with intellectual disabilities (ID), including prompt formulation difficulties, response overload, and limited mechanisms to assess information reliability. To explore alternative interaction models for cognitive accessibility, we conducted a cross-disciplinary co-design challenge in which two student cohorts (Computer Science and Industrial Design) developed interface concepts from the same set of functional requirements (e.g., prompt scaffolding, structured output, GUI-based refinement, transparency, and personalization). Comparing the resulting proposals reveals both convergence on foundational requirements (notably initial calibration, proactive prompting, and direct manipulation of response fragments) and complementary contributions that outline a multi-layered support system. Computer Science teams primarily produced structural scaffolding, emphasizing predictability, navigability, and trust through mechanisms such as reliability indicators, explicit sources, and context management for long conversations. Industrial Design teams emphasized experiential scaffolding, focusing on pacing, attention guidance, multimodality, and proactive agency, including step-by-step response flows, focus modes, and assistant-like integrations. We synthesize these findings into a dual-layer scaffolding framework that expands the design space for cognitively accessible GenAI interaction beyond chat-centric models and motivates future work on expert refinement, technical feasibility, and empirical validation with users with ID.
2606.14306.paper_summary_zh: 目前的生成式人工智慧（GenAI）介面仍然主要限於聊天框互動，這對使用者造成高認知負擔，並為有智力障礙（ID）的人士創造了相當大的障礙，包括提示制定困難、反應過載以及評估信息可靠性的機制有限。為了探索認知可及性的替代互動模型，我們進行了一項跨學科的共同設計挑戰，兩個學生群體（計算機科學和工業設計）根據相同的一組功能需求（例如提示支架、結構化輸出、基於GUI的精煉、透明度和個性化）開發介面概念。比較所產生的提案顯示出在基礎需求上的趨同（特別是初步校準、主動提示和對反應片段的直接操作）以及互補的貢獻，勾勒出一個多層次的支持系統。計算機科學團隊主要產生結構性支架，強調可預測性、可導航性和通過可靠性指標、明確來源和長時間對話的上下文管理等機制來建立信任。工業設計團隊則強調經驗性支架，專注於節奏、注意力引導、多模態和主動代理，包括逐步反應流程、焦點模式和助手式整合。我們將這些發現綜合成一個雙層支架框架，擴展了認知可及的GenAI互動的設計空間，超越了以聊天為中心的模型，並激勵未來在專家精煉、技術可行性和與有智力障礙的使用者進行實證驗證方面的工作。
2606.14306.author: Virginia Francisco et.al.
2606.14306.authors: Virginia Francisco, Daniel Guasch, Raquel Hervás
